name: Dannie Richmond
abstract: Charles Daniel Richmond (December 15, 1931 – March 16, 1988) was an American jazz drummer who is best known for his work with Charles Mingus. He also worked with Joe Cocker, Elton John and Mark-Almond.

concerts.label: The Charles Mingus Quintet featuring Dizzy Gillespie, Trumpet
An Evening with Charles Mingus and Old Friends

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: So, it was all brass plus saxophone, and Danny Richmond and Mingus.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: And this was all part of his quote unquote show. Okay? “You guys are not playing shit. Get out of here. You’re fired. You’re all fired. Y’all stay.” And then the © Fillius Jazz Archive -8- small group would play with Lonnie and Charles McPherson and Danny Richmond.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Akiyoshi Toshiko
about_quotes.evidence: Mingus and Dannie Richmond were more like they were a couple... And Dannie was a very nice man. He was a really nice man, and Lew had some trips [with him] in Canada, a couple times, together. But he loved duck, and I cooked the duck here sometimes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Akiyoshi Toshiko — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Roland Hanna
about_quotes.evidence: And it was a good thing that he had Danny Richmond with him for so long because Danny was with him like they were like on water skis going together... Wherever we played... we usually rehearsed three or four hours.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roland Hanna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 15, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Reggie Watkins
about_quotes.evidence: Danny Richmond, Eric Dolphy and Jimmy Knepper. So we went and did this big concert... I had only known Jimmy — I never had a Jimmy Knepper record... I had all the Mingus stuff because I’m a Mingus fanatic
about_quotes.doc_title: Reggie Watkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, May 22, 2018

about_quotes.speaker: Toots Thielemans
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: And then I saw that you had done a date with Dannie Richmond... [INTERVIEWEE] No, but my revelation was we’re not [inaudible].
about_quotes.doc_title: Toots Thielemans — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
